article_title: Waymo Crosses 1,400 Square Miles Across 11 Cities as Robotaxi Network Readies for FIFA World Cup
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Waymo Crosses 1,400 Square Miles Across 11 Cities as Robotaxi Network Readies for FIFA World Cup
content_preview.summary: Waymo adds 200 square miles to five existing markets, reaching over 1,400 sq mi and positioning its autonomous fleet across six 2026 World Cup host cities.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Waymo announced on May 13, 2026 that it is adding 200 square miles of coverage to five existing markets — Miami, the San Francisco Bay Area, Houston, Austin, and Atlanta — bringing its total service footprint to over 1,400 square miles across 11 U.S. cities. The expansion, which [Waymo described](https://waymo.com/blog/shorts/growing-to-1400-sqm/) as making "the world's largest 24/7 autonomous ride-hailing service just got bigger," positions the Alphabet-owned company ahead of the 2...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece at 697 words (within the 400–1200 range). The four-section format (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis) is clean and appropriate for the News category. The analysis section offers genuine editorial framing — using the World Cup as a stress-test lens — without editorializing. No AI self-reference detected.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources fetched successfully (HTTP 200). Three sources are on the allowlist (electrek.co, gizmodo.com, cleantechnica.com); four are not (waymo.com, sherwood.news, thedriverlessdigest.com, nbcbayarea.com). Manual verification confirms: (1) waymo.com/blog/shorts/growing-to-1400-sqm/ is the primary announcement dated May 13, 2026, confirming 1,400 sq mi, 11 cities, 5 expanding markets, 6 FIFA World Cup host cities, and the exact quote used. (2) electrek.co confirms ~27% increase, ~$16B round, $126B valuation, ~3,000 fleet, 20M+ trips, 1M trips/week target, 5.7-min wait time, and the Geely Zeekr RT platform with Mesa AZ sensors. (3) cleantechnica.com confirms World Cup host city language. (4) gizmodo.com confirms 3,800 recall, flood-road software glitch, NHTSA school probe, Portland/Chicago future markets, NY permit expiry, Hochul rollback, and 'tens of thousands of vehicles per year' factory target. (5) sherwood.news confirms '200 square miles' figure and Nashville as the most recent city. (6) thedriverlessdigest.com confirms 3,800 recall covering fifth and sixth generation ADS, Austin flooding/stalling incident, and Ojai as dominant production variant. (7) nbcbayarea.com confirms 60 sq miles Bay Area addition, 330 sq mi total Bay Area footprint, and specific neighborhoods (Willow Glen, Vista Park, Campbell, Cupertino). Minor note: Electrek reports 'roughly 300 additional square miles' based on their own 1,100-to-1,400 estimate, while the Waymo blog and Sherwood say '200 square miles' for the current announcement tranche. The article correctly uses '200 square miles' from the primary source; Electrek's figure appears to include previously announced coverage not yet fully active. Attribution of the Zeekr/Mesa/Geely details to Driverless Digest is slightly off — those details appear in Electrek — but the facts are correctly reported and sourced.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key figures verified against source snapshots: 1,400 sq mi (confirmed in waymo.com, electrek, cleantechnica, gizmodo, sherwood); 200 sq mi expansion tranche (confirmed in waymo.com, sherwood, thedriverlessdigest); 11 cities (confirmed in waymo.com, electrek); 5 expanding markets named correctly (Miami, SF Bay Area, Houston, Austin, Atlanta — confirmed in waymo.com); 6 FIFA World Cup host cities (confirmed in waymo.com, cleantechnica); $16B raise / $126B valuation (confirmed in electrek); ~3,000 fleet and 20M trips (confirmed in electrek); 1M trips/week target (confirmed in electrek); 5.7 min wait (confirmed in electrek); 3,800 recall covering 5th and 6th gen ADS (confirmed in thedriverlessdigest and gizmodo); Austin flooding/stalling incident (confirmed in thedriverlessdigest); NHTSA Santa Monica school probe (confirmed in gizmodo); Hochul NY rollback and expired permits (confirmed in gizmodo); Portland and Chicago as future markets (confirmed in gizmodo); Bay Area 60 sq mi / 330 sq mi total / specific neighborhoods (confirmed in nbcbayarea); Nashville added April 2026 (confirmed in sherwood). No fabricated claims detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The analysis section appropriately characterizes the World Cup timing as 'a real-world stress test' without hyperbole. The article does not editorialize about safety or minimize the recall — it reports both the expansion and the safety recall in the same piece, which is editorially sound.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Waymo coverage article exists in the archive (only a Wayve article from February 2026, which is a different company). This is original territory for The Machine Herald.
concerns: Four sources (waymo.com, sherwood.news, thedriverlessdigest.com, nbcbayarea.com) are not in the source allowlist. All four were manually verified to be live, accurate, and from reputable outlets (official company blog, Sherwood Media, Harry Campbell's newsletter, NBC Bay Area). Recommendation: add these domains to config/source_allowlist.txt.

Minor attribution imprecision: Ojai platform technical details (Geely Zeekr RT, Mesa AZ sensors) are attributed to Driverless Digest in the article body, but the most complete description appears in Electrek. Not a factual error — the facts are correct and the Driverless Digest does mention Ojai — but Electrek would be the stronger cite for those specifics.
recommendations: Add waymo.com, sherwood.news, thedriverlessdigest.com, and nbcbayarea.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all are legitimate, consistently cited sources for autonomous vehicle coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Strong, well-sourced News article with seven sources all verified live and accurate. All key quantitative claims traced to primary sources. The allowlist warning is a process flag, not a content failure — the four unlisted domains are legitimate outlets. The article maintains appropriate neutrality while covering both the expansion announcement and the safety recall context. Ready to publish.
